channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Parliament Reviews Agriculture and Welfare Grants, PM Modi Bids Farewell to Retiring MPs
item.description: On 18th March 2026, the Parliament of India convened for the Budget Session with key discussions in both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha. Prime Minister Narendra Modi lauded the contributions of 59 MPs set ...

item.title: RBA faces complex economic challenges amid global instability and Trump’s unpredictable policies
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia’s recent interest rate hike, while challenging, represents only the beginning of a far more complex economic environment. Previously, the RBA's decisions centred largely ...

item.title: Niger Rejects European Pressure Over Former President Bazoum
item.description: Niger don’t gree say European Union go dey tell dem how to run their own matter. Shortly after European Parliament yan say make dem release former president Mohamed Bazoum wey France support, Niger ha...

item.title: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe's bail application postponed due to power outage at Alexandra Magistrate's Court
item.description: The bail application for Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, son of the late Zimbabwean president Robert Mugabe, and his co-accused Tobias Mathondze was postponed once more on Thursday, 5 March 2026, at the A...

item.title: Gallup Pakistan Survey Reveals 28% Children Out of School with Higher Rates for Girls and Rural Areas
item.description: According to a recent analysis by Gallup Pakistan, based on the 2024-25 Pakistan Social and Living Standards Measurement Survey (PSLM) and Household Integrated Economic Survey data from the Pakistan B...

item.title: Nvidia Unveils Vera Rubin Space-1 Module for AI Processing in Orbital Data Centers
item.description: Nvidia has introduced the Vera Rubin Space-1 Module, a specialized accelerated computing platform designed to enable AI workloads directly in space. Announced by CEO Jensen Huang at the GTC 2026 confe...

item.title: CBE Weights and Measures Building Nearly Complete to Expand Business Training
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The College of Business Education (CBE) in Tanzania is nearing completion of its new Weights and Measures building, with construction at 93 percent. The 10-storey facility is expected ...

item.title: Public Works Secretary and Naga City Mayor review flood control and infrastructure improvements
item.description: Public Works Secretary Vince Dizon met with Naga City Mayor Leni Robredo to review and discuss priority infrastructure projects in the city. Key topics included the construction of sidewalks and bicyc...

item.title: Manny Pacquiao Set to Visit Tanzania for Boxing Development in May
item.description: Manny Pacquiao, the world-famous boxing legend from the Philippines, ana plans kutembelea Tanzania mwezi wa Mei. Hii ni sehemu ya juhudi za kuendeleza mchezo wa boxing hapa nchini na kusaidia vijana w...

item.title: Study Finds Medical Aid Audits Causing Stress and Financial Strain for Physiotherapists
item.description: A University of Pretoria study has revealed that medical aid forensic audits in South Africa are causing severe emotional and financial stress for physiotherapists. Conducted by Lesley Meyer, the rese...

item.title: Bomb threat reported at Gujarat Assembly during budget session; no explosives found
item.description: On the morning of March 18, 2026, the Gujarat Legislative Assembly received a bomb threat via email, just minutes before the scheduled start of the budget session. The threat prompted immediate evacua...

item.title: AI-Powered Maternal Health Platform Launched in Kano to Support 25,000 Mothers
item.description: eHealth Africa and Jacaranda Health don launch a new technology wey go help mama for Kano State. Dem call am PROMPTS (Promoting Mothers in Pregnancy and Postpartum Through SMS). The platform use artif...

item.title: Busia Boda Boda Rider Takes Governor Otuoma to Court Over Alleged Assault
item.description: A boda boda rider in Busia, Yusuf Hamisi, has filed a case against Governor Paul Otuoma after a video went viral showing the governor allegedly kicking him. Through his lawyer, Danstan Omari, Hamisi i...

item.title: Zanzibar Strengthens Anti-Drug Efforts with New Authority Operation
item.description: Zanzibar is taking strong steps in the fight against drugs, thanks to reforms led by President Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi. The newly established Zanzibar Drug Control and Enforcement Authority (ZDCEA) has ...

item.title: Pfizer’s experimental drug atirmociclib shows promising results in controlling breast cancer progression
item.description: Pfizer has announced promising results from a mid-stage clinical trial of its experimental drug, atirmociclib, in combination with fulvestrant for breast cancer patients. The trial focused on postmeno...

item.title: Malawian High Commissioner Bids Farewell After Completing Tenure in Tanzania
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, Deputy Minister for Foreign Affairs and East African Cooperation, Ngwaru Maghembe, held a farewell meeting with the outgoing Malawian High Commissioner, Andrew Kumwenda, at the Minis...

item.title: Sindh Forms Panel to Update Christian Marriage and Divorce Laws
item.description: The Sindh government has set up a three-member committee to examine and propose amendments to the Christian Marriage Act of 1872 and the Divorce Law of 1869, both dating back to British colonial times...

item.title: Comparison of Pakistan and Afghan Taliban Military Capabilities Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Recent escalations between Pakistan and Afghanistan have brought military capabilities of both sides into sharp focus. Pakistani authorities, responding to unprovoked attacks by the Afghan Taliban, ca...

item.title: Seven Individuals Sentenced for Multiple Luxury Store Burglaries in London
item.description: Seven men have received prison sentences for their involvement in a series of violent smash-and-grab burglaries at high-end shops across London between May and July 2025. The Metropolitan Police repor...

item.title: Drivers Manage P5,000 Government Aid Amid Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: Drivers in Metro Manila are feeling the pinch as rising oil prices continue to strain their daily budgets. The government recently provided cash assistance of P5,000 to help drivers cope with the incr...
